title: STM TVar.read() outside transaction is runtime-only error — type system cannot enforce
description: TVar.read() 'returns an error if called outside a transaction context' rather than being enforced by the type system. Code that reads a TVar outside atomically() compiles fine. The error is only discovered at runtime. Haskell's STM prevents this statically via the STM monad. Zinc's lack of effect system means the call-inside-transaction rule cannot be enforced. What caller observes: unexpected error.STMNotInTransaction at runtime, often deep in call chains.
